steps.content: DataStore allows you to save player data permanently. This means progress won't be lost when players leave and rejoin.

steps.instruction: Create a simple data saving system:
steps.starterCode:
-- Create a DataStore
-- Save some player data
-- Print confirmation
steps.solution:
local DataStoreService = game:GetService("DataStoreService")
local playerDataStore = DataStoreService:GetDataStore("PlayerData")

local playerData = {
  coins = 150,
  level = 3,
  lastPlayed = os.time()
}

local success, result = pcall(function()
  playerDataStore:SetAsync("Player123", playerData)
end)

if success then
  print("Data saved successfully!")
else
  print("Error saving data: " .. result)
end
steps.hint: Use pcall() to safely handle DataStore operations

steps.instruction: Create a function to load player data:
steps.starterCode:
-- Create a function to load player data
-- Handle errors gracefully
-- Print the loaded data
steps.solution:
local function loadPlayerData(playerId)
  local success, data = pcall(function()
    return playerDataStore:GetAsync(playerId)
  end)
  
  if success and data then
    print("Loaded data for player " .. playerId)
    print("Coins: " .. data.coins)
    print("Level: " .. data.level)
    return data
  else
    print("No data found for player " .. playerId)
    return nil
  end
end

loadPlayerData("Player123")
steps.hint: Use GetAsync() to retrieve data from DataStore

successMessage: 💾 Your data persistence skills are top-notch!
